traversal_convention.container_rule: S3.2: a node with a traverse disposition consumes its child words, word-parts, and role-covered children directly within that role, and it recurses into nested statement-bearing parts (for example, a CmdSubst embedded inside a Word) without re-dispatching those children through this table.
traversal_convention.wildcard_rule: S3.2: a row with role "*" applies only when the node is encountered in a role not covered by any explicit (node, role) row in this table, so a wildcard refuse row never overrides in-role consumption performed by a traverse container.

rows.spec: S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The file body is the top-level statement list that hosts every source.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. A statement wraps one command plus its redirects; both are walked in source order.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The argv words are the command site the certifier emits.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The && || and | operands are statements; a pipeline is modeled here via Stmt.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. A brace block runs its body in the current shell.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. A subshell runs its body statements as real execution sources.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. A command substitution hosts statements that execute.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. A function body is a statement-bearing execution source.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The condition and then/elif/else bodies are executed statements.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The while or until condition and loop body are executed statements.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The loop body plus the selector words are executed sources and condition words.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The case selector word can host a command substitution.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. Each case item runs its body statements; its pattern words can host expansions.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. The for-in selector words are condition and selector words per S3.2.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. A redirect target word expansion can host a command substitution.
S3.2 traverse: statement-bearing execution source. An unquoted-delimiter heredoc body expands and can host an execution source.
Owner-ratified 2026-07-21 during Task 8 Tier 3B adjudication: assignment-value word-parts are consumed in-role and embedded CmdSubst recurses, exactly as argv; ratified against S3.2.
S3.2 ignore: provably cannot host an execution source. A comment is inert text and cannot host an execution source.
S3.2 ignore: provably cannot host an execution source. Single-quoted content is literal data with no expansion.
S3.2 ignore: provably cannot host an execution source. A quoted-delimiter heredoc body is literal data with no expansion.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An arithmetic command (( )) is an unsupported execution semantic.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An arithmetic expansion operand is not statically resolvable in general.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The arithmetic-expression interface covers operands the certifier does not model.

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An array element value can host a command substitution but is refused fail-closed pending corpus evaluation (uncertain: could be traverse).

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An array literal can host a command substitution but is refused fail-closed pending corpus evaluation (uncertain: could be traverse).

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An assignment encountered in a role other than value is refused fail-closed; the value role is separately covered by its own explicit traverse row (owner-ratified 2026-07-21).
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An arithmetic binary operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A binary arithmetic node is an unmodeled arithmetic operand.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A binary test node inside [[ ]] is an unmodeled construct.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A binary command operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A test binary operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A brace expansion can widen a word to many arguments; refused fail-closed.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A case terminator operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The command interface default arm refuses every unmodeled command node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A coproc clause is an unsupported execution semantic.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A C-style for (( )) loop header is an unmodeled arithmetic construct.

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A double-quoted word can host a command substitution but is refused fail-closed pending corpus evaluation (uncertain: could be traverse).
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A declare/local/export clause is an unsupported execution semantic.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parameter-expansion operator part is not statically resolvable in general.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An extended glob is an unmodeled matching construct.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. An arithmetic flags node is an unmodeled arithmetic operand.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A glob operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A language-variant error type is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A language-variant enum is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A let clause is an unsupported arithmetic execution semantic.

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. Literal argv text is collected under a traversed command site; a standalone literal is refused fail-closed (uncertain: inert, could be ignore).
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The loop interface default arm refuses every unmodeled loop header.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The node interface default arm refuses every unknown or future node kind.

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parameter expansion is collected as an argv word fact under a traversed command site; standalone it is refused fail-closed (uncertain: could be traverse).
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parenthesized arithmetic node is an unmodeled arithmetic operand.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parenthesized test node is an unmodeled construct.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parameter-expansion operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parameter-names operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parse-error type is not a syntax node; a parse failure is a syntax-error refusal.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The parser struct is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parser-option function type is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A position value is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The printer struct is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A printer-option function type is not a syntax node.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A process-substitution operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A process substitution spawns a process and is an unsupported execution semantic.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A quote-error type is not a syntax node; it is a syntax-error refusal.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A redirect operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parameter-replacement part is not statically resolvable in general.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A parameter-slice part is not statically resolvable in general.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A test clause [[ ]] is an unsupported execution semantic.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A test declaration is an unmodeled construct.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The test-expression interface covers unmodeled test operands.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A time clause is an unsupported execution semantic.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A unary arithmetic operator enum is not an execution source.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A unary arithmetic node is an unmodeled arithmetic operand.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A unary test node is an unmodeled construct.
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A test unary operator enum is not an execution source.

S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. A word is collected under a traversed command, redirect, or selector context; a standalone word is refused fail-closed (uncertain: could be traverse).
S3.2 refuse: fail-closed default for a construct outside the traverse and ignore sets. The word-part interface default arm refuses every unmodeled word part.
